snippets.pull_quote: Of the five projects I'm running — what is where, what is my next best action, what is waiting, what is late? I need a super integrated project management list across the whole ecosystem.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to fractional operators drowning in multi-client context-switching with no single source of truth.

snippets.pull_quote: The idea of the command center is probably the most appealing thing about it for me. Every other tool tells me they're the command center too — so you've got to figure out some way of getting proof points.
snippets.website_angle: Position as the command center that actually delivers — and lead with proof, not promises.

snippets.pull_quote: I sent out a thousand messages and booked 10 meetings — that's a painful psychological experience when I know it's just how the world works.
snippets.website_angle: Acknowledge the emotional cost of BD for solo operators; position tool as filtering signal from noise.

snippets.pull_quote: All I see now is a meeting has been set. It's all yeses as far as I'm concerned — all I get are the endorphin hits of four meetings booked tomorrow.
snippets.website_angle: Frame the product around surfacing wins and momentum, not just task management.

snippets.pull_quote: My problem has been one foot in and one foot out of this world — do I go back into fractional or stay full-time? That's an unusual problem but it's real.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to operators in transition who need infrastructure that works whether they're scaling up or winding down.

snippets.pull_quote: They're trying to say: how do I take this idea that is currently me and my network and scale it into a business that grows and repeats?
snippets.website_angle: Use as framing for the core ICP pain — the founder who is the marketing motion and needs to escape that trap.

snippets.pull_quote: Many of my peers are in marketing because they don't understand sales. They finish a client meeting and say 'thank you for your time, we'll be in touch' — and never asked for anything.
snippets.website_angle: Surface for messaging around helping fractional CMOs close, not just deliver.

snippets.pull_quote: He said $600 a month is a lot of money. I said you're completely wrong. If your retainer is $5,000 or $10,000, this is nothing.
snippets.website_angle: Use to reframe price objections — small tool spend vs. one retainer won makes the math obvious.

snippets.pull_quote: You effectively attempt to become invisible and white label it — maybe this becomes a dashboard I share with my clients on update calls.
snippets.website_angle: Tease a client-facing shared view as a premium use case for fractional operators running multiple accounts.

snippets.pull_quote: As you think about how you're describing this — you want to start talking about proof points as soon as you can get there. I think that's the elephant in the room.
snippets.website_angle: Direct signal to lead website with case evidence and outcomes, not feature lists.

snippets.pull_quote: If I start getting further into feast mode, I have a plan to subcontract with other fractional CMOs — we're roundtabling on Mondays on client problems. This sort of tool would be helpful managing that.
snippets.website_angle: Position for the fractional operator growing into a small pod or agency model needing lightweight team coordination.

snippets.pull_quote: It's cracking the code on go-to-market. The specific nature of that challenge is different nearly every time — which my ADHD brain thinks is awesome.
snippets.website_angle: Mirror the language of operators who thrive on variety but need structure underneath the chaos.
